reasoning: The Lean statement ∀ N, ∃ p ≥ N, p prime ∧ p + 1 ≡ 0 [MOD 4] is the standard encoding of "infinitely many primes p satisfying the property", since a subset of ℕ is infinite iff it is unbounded. The condition `p + 1 ≡ 0 [MOD 4]`, given Mathlib's definition of `Nat.ModEq n a b` as `a % n = b % n`, is equivalent to `4 ∣ (p + 1)`, which is precisely `p ≡ -1 (mod 4)`. Hypotheses and quantifier structure match the informal claim exactly, with no added or dropped assumptions.
